name: Protect GraphQL Introspection and Schema Enumeration
objective: Detect and block high-signal GraphQL introspection and schema enumeration requests on production-facing GraphQL endpoints.
risk_mitigated: Schema discovery that accelerates abuse of hidden operations, undocumented object paths, and weak authorization controls.
potential_side_effects: Developer tooling, CI schema validation, and staging environments may legitimately use introspection. Production use requires explicit exceptions for trusted automation.
tuning_notes: Start in log mode and scope the rule to production GraphQL paths only. Exclude internal IP ranges, service tokens, or staging hostnames instead of weakening the core expression.
deployment_notes: Deploy on GraphQL entrypoints such as /graphql and /api/graphql. Keep developer playgrounds and staging hostnames out of production zones whenever possible. Promote from log to managed challenge after validating legitimate clients.
monitoring_notes: Review Cloudflare Security Events for repeated introspection attempts, user-agents tied to scanners, and partners or internal tooling that still depend on schema discovery. Track top source ASNs and top paths.
cloudflare_expression: (http.request.uri.path in {"/graphql" "/api/graphql"} and (http.request.body contains "__schema" or http.request.body contains "__type" or http.request.body contains "IntrospectionQuery"))
log_only_expression: (http.request.uri.path in {"/graphql" "/api/graphql"} and (http.request.body contains "__schema" or http.request.body contains "__type" or http.request.body contains "IntrospectionQuery"))
